sections.title: Definition of produce
sections.text: This standard applies to commercial varieties of bananas grown from Musa spp. of the Musaceae family, in the green state, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Bananas intended for cooking only (plantains) or for industrial processing are excluded. Varieties covered by this standard are included in the annex.

sections.title: “Extra” class
sections.text: Bananas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The fingers must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality, and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text:
Bananas in this class mus t be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects of the fingers, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:
- slight defects in shape and colour; and
- slight skin defects due to rubbing and other superficial defects not exceeding 2 cm2 of the total surface area.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the flesh of the fruit.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: For the purposes of sizing bananas of the Gros Michel and Cavendish subgroups, the length of the fingers is determined along the outside curve from the blossom end to the base of the pedicel where the edible pulp ends, and the diameter is defined as the thickness of a transverse section between the lateral faces. The reference fruit for measurement of the length and grade is: for hands, the median finger on the outer row of the hand; and for clusters, the finger next to the cut section of the hand, on the outer row of the cluster. The minimum length should not be less than 14 cm and the minimum grade not less than 2.7 cm.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Bananas must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly . The materials used inside the package must be new,i clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Bananas shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).1 For the purposes of this standard, this includes recycled material of food-grade quality.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes bananas which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section  2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the bananas retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality, and presentation: defects in shape and colour, provided the product retains the normal characteristics of bananas; and skin defects due to scraping, scabs, rubbing, blemishes, or other causes, not exceeding 4 cm2 of the total surface area. The defects must not, in any case, affect the flesh of the fruit.

sections.title: Presentation
sections.text: The bananas must be presented in hands and clusters (parts of hands) of at least four fingers. Bananas may also be presented as single fingers. Clusters with no more than two missing fingers are allowed, provided the stalk is not torn but cleanly cut, without damage to the neighbouring fingers. Not more than one cluster of three fingers with the same characteristics as the other fruit in the package may be present per row.

classification.function: Specification of quality requirements and conditions for acceptance
classification.pattern: General statement introducing subject and applicability conditions; followed by a list of obligatory quality and condition traits for main item (bananas) and additional parts (hands and clusters), each item expressed as an adjective phrase or descriptive clause; includes exceptions and references to tolerances and special provisions; uses modal verb 'must' to impose requirements; list format employed for clarity of multiple sub-requirements

components.subject: bananas
components.applicability: in all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed
requirements_main_item: whole (taking the finger as the reference)
sound (produce affected by rotting or deterioration such as to make it unfit for consumption is excluded)
clean (practically free of any visible foreign matter)
practically free of pests affecting the general appearance of the produce
practically free of damage caused by pests
free of abnormal external moisture (excluding condensation following removal from cold storage, and bananas packed under modified atmosphere conditions)
free of any foreign smell and/or taste
free of damage caused by low temperatures
free of malformation or abnormal curvature of the fingers
with the stalk intact (without bending, fungal damage or desiccation)
requirements_additional_parts: a sufficient portion of the crown of normal colouring, sound and free of fungal contamination
a cleanly cut crown, not bevelled or torn, with no stalk fragments

classification.justification: The sentence contains an obligation modality expressed by 'must' and sets mandatory quality requirements for bananas and their parts. It uses a detailed enumerated list structure with complex adjectival and exclusionary descriptions. The communicative function is to specify acceptability standards. No existing templates are available, and the complex multi-layered list of required attributes reflects a pattern not represented in the known template catalog, justifying a new pattern classification.
notes: The sentence is complex with multiple nested clauses and exceptions.
The enumeration of diverse quality attributes, some with exceptions, creates a unique structural pattern not common in simpler obligation templates.

classification.justification: The sentence defines a classification scheme for 'Bananas' using a passive construction and references classifications defined later, which does not match any known template; modality is obligation as it mandates classification, communicative function is definitional statement.
notes: The classification category is introduced without an explicit normative verb like 'shall' but implies a mandatory framework.
Sentence is structurally unique and introduces a new pattern for classification statements.

classification.function: Specification of quality or performance criteria
classification.pattern: List of infinitive verb phrases specifying conditions or criteria to be met

criteria.description: to reach the appropriate stage of physiological maturity corresponding to the particular characteristics of the variety
to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination in order to ripen satisfactorily

classification.justification: The sentence is a list of conditions or criteria expressed as infinitive verb phrases describing desired states or qualities. There is no direct obligation, permission, prohibition, or fixed quantitative limit expressed, so the best modality is Condition with a communicative function focused on specifying quality or performance criteria. The structure is a coordinated list of infinitive clauses, a pattern not present in the known template catalog, thus a new pattern is created.
notes: The sentence is fragmentary (a list) and does not standalone as a complete normative statement, which suggests it is part of a larger context outlining conditions or requirements.

notes: The phrasing 'should not be less than' may cause slight ambiguity between obligation and recommendation, but here it is interpreted normatively as minimum requirements.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR BANANAS
pages.summary: A página contém o título principal do documento e o cabeçalho padrão, além de duas seções principais não numeradas e uma seção numerada '1. 1. Definition of produce' com texto explicativo. Nenhuma seção apresenta conteúdo continuado de outras páginas.

A página contém os títulos principais numerados das seções 1 e 2 com suas subseções. O texto está distribuído apropriadamente, com continuações indicadas para seções que seguem na próxima página, especialmente nas classes da classificação. O cabeçalho padrão foi identificado e excluído do texto dos nós para manter qualidade para RAG.

Página estrutural com títulos numerados de nível 1 a 3, incluindo continuidade indicada em subseção 2.2.3 de página anterior. O texto está distribuído adequadamente e isento do cabeçalho repetitivo para qualidade em RAG.

A página contém seções e subseções numeradas organizadas hierarquicamente dos níveis 1 a 3, delimitando temas sobre containers, apresentação, marcação, contaminantes e higiene. O texto está limpo de cabeçalhos repetitivos e as seções não demonstram continuidade para páginas seguintes.

pages.title: CXS 205-1997
pages.summary: A página apresenta dois títulos principais: 'ANNEX' com uma lista detalhada de grupos e cultivares de bananas para sobremesa e 'NOTES' com referências normativas. Não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas. O texto interno está associado corretamente aos seus títulos, com títulos importantes em caixa alta e sem numeração clara, tratados como nível 1.
